message:
feat(messaging): semantic degradation policy for FABRIC lane overload decisions

Replace DeliveryPolicyPlaceholder stub with a full semantic degradation
policy engine that makes overload decisions based on workload meaning
rather than raw queue depth alone.

SemanticServiceClass (8 classes):
- ControlRecovery, LeaseRepair, ReplyCritical, DurablePipeline,
  Interactive, ReadModel, LowValueFanout, ExpensiveReplay
- base_priority() for static ordering, uses_reserved_capacity() for
  control/lease traffic

ObligationLoad:
- None, Reply, Lease, ReplyAndLease with priority_boost()

TrafficSlice:
- Combines service class, obligation load, delivery class, and
  inflight/pending counts into a scorable unit
- effective_priority() = base + obligation_boost + delivery_boost

DegradationPolicy:
- overload_threshold, critical_threshold, reserved_capacity_fraction
- evaluate() produces DegradationDecision (Admit/Degrade/Shed/Block)
  based on load ratio and class-specific rules
- Reserved capacity protects control/lease traffic during shedding

DegradationPlan:
- Multi-slice degradation plan with per-slice dispositions
- plan_degradation() evaluates all slices and produces the plan
- DegradationDisposition: Admit, Degrade(reason), Shed(reason)

Tests (15 tests):
- Priority ordering across all service classes
- Obligation boost effects, delivery class boost
- Policy evaluation at all load levels
- Reserved capacity preservation during shedding
- Multi-slice plan generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
